name: Beef Scallops (with Fresh Tomato Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. About 45 minutes before serving, pound each beef top round steak to 1/8-inch thickness using a meat mallet or the dull edge of a French knife on a cutting board.
2. Cut steaks into about 6 x 3-inch pieces.
3. In a 3-quart saucepan over high heat, bring water to boiling. Add tomatoes and cook for 30 seconds.
4. Using a slotted spoon, remove tomatoes to a bowl of cold water. Drain and peel the skins off. Remove stems from tomatoes and chop 3 of them. Slice the remaining 2.
5. In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat in hot salad oil, brown steak pieces in batches using a pancake turner. Transfer browned steaks to a warm platter.
6. Reduce heat to medium. Add chopped tomatoes, green onions (if used), sugar, salt, and basil to the skillet. In a separate cup with a spoon, mix cornstarch with 1/2 cup water until smooth.
7. Gradually stir the cornstarch mixture into the tomato mixture, cooking constantly until it thickens and boils for 1 minute.
8. Add sliced tomatoes; heat through. Gently spoon the tomato mixture over and around the steaks.
9. Makes 8 servings.
